title: GCBF future round watch
notify: Not-for-profit clubs, community groups, sport groups, arts groups and auspiced informal groups.
tip: Round 126 is closed and the next round date is not announced yet. Use the waiting period to confirm eligibility, auspice, quotes, public benefit and reporting capacity.
action: Prepare profile pack, project budget and quote list before the next opening is announced.

title: Redland Council community project readiness
notify: Community groups, environment groups, artists, clubs and local project leads.
tip: Council grants care about public benefit, value for money, delivery skill, risk control, milestone reporting and final acquittal. Do not wait until the portal deadline to collect evidence.
action: Build grant-readiness-checklist.md and milestone-report.md before applying.
source: Redland City Council Community Support Grant

title: First Nations opportunity check
notify: First Nations organisations, cultural projects, Country-care projects and partner-led projects where cultural authority is clear.
tip: Check who is actually eligible before drafting. Some programs are restricted, invited or require Indigenous governance. Cultural permission is not a writing detail; it is a project foundation.
action: Confirm applicant authority, partner role, permissions and public wording before any notice is published.
source: Indigenous.gov.au and Queensland First Nations grants pages

title: Sport infrastructure and 2032 legacy scan
notify: Sport clubs, facility committees, surf, beach sport, youth and inclusive recreation projects.
tip: Sport grants often need site control, quotes, facility plans, participation evidence, maintenance responsibility and inclusion benefits.
action: Keep a sport facility evidence folder and update it monthly.

title: Business support scan
notify: Local businesses, sole traders, artists, food producers, tourism operators and digital service providers.
tip: Business grants usually filter by location, business stage, industry, Indigenous business status, activity type and whether the help is funding, advice, loan or rebate.
action: Use business.gov.au filters and keep business-profile.md current.

title: Global environment and island resilience watch
notify: Environment, biodiversity, climate, land-and-sea, women-led, Indigenous peoples and research partnership projects.
tip: Global grant pathways usually need stronger partnerships, evidence, governance and impact language. They suit long preparation windows better than last-minute applications.
action: Treat as partnership development first, application writing second.

title: Closing soon triage rule
notify: Only entities that match the grant type and already have enough evidence to apply.
tip: A closing-soon notice should not create panic for everyone. It should say who is likely eligible, what is still needed and whether the deadline is realistic.
action: Send targeted notice, not whole-island noise.
